input.headline: The AI Shift: Do we prefer talking to machines rather than each other?

raw.headline: The AI Shift: Do we prefer talking to machines rather than each other?

winner: Why did the AI break up with its human partner? It said, "I'm just not into *me* anymore—turns out I'm more of a *self*-love than a *human*-love.

candidates.text: Why did my therapist upgrade to an AI app? Turns out, I was just looking for someone who doesn't interrupt me and still charges by the minute.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on therapy and AI, specific context with humor in the billing detail"}

candidates.text: I asked my smart speaker for advice on my failing relationship. It said, "Your partner is 20% more likely to leave you than a toaster." I replied, "Can you at least tell me if she left the oven on?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific, relatable scenario; concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: Why did I stop talking to people? I realized they couldn't tell me my battery life was at 23%—they'd just say, 'You're at 100% of your potential!'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on AI capabilities, specific and concise with a relatable tech reference."}

candidates.text: Why did the AI break up with its human partner? It said, "I'm just not into *me* anymore—turns out I'm more of a *self*-love than a *human*-love.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'self-love' and 'human-love' are clever and specific, with a surprising twist that ties back to the AI theme. Brevity and natural English are well executed."}

candidates.text: Sure, I can have a nice conversation with my smart fridge—it never interrupts or asks why I ate the last slice of pizza at 3 AM.
